entry.title: NY Times
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Trump, Told It Was Illegal, Still Pressured Pence to Overturn His Loss
  • For Mike Pence, Jan. 6 Began Like Many Days. It Ended Like No Other.
  • Near Embattled Ukrainian Cities, a Mass Grave for Civilians Grows
  • France and Germany Stand With Ukraine, and Putin Can Wait
  • As European Leaders Visit Kyiv, Putin Cuts Their Gas Supply
  • Stock Markets Fall After Fed Rally Fades
  • Mortgage Rates Jump to 5.78 Percent, Rising at the Fastest Pace Since 1987
  • A Recession Would Hurt Democrats. Some Warn It’d Also Hurt Democracy.
  • Fears Grow Over Iran’s Nuclear Program as Tehran Digs a New Tunnel Network
  • Hochul Spars With Rivals Over Crime, Credentials and Cream Cheese
  • 5 Takeaways From the NY Governor Debate
  • ‘The Old Man’ Brought Jeff Bridges to TV. John Lithgow Had No Advice.
  • Don’t Let Bill Barr and Ivanka Trump Visit the Reputation Laundromat
  • Yes, Crypto Is Crashing Again. Blockchain Will Survive.
  • In France’s Elections, Something Extraordinary Is Happening
  • What Is Golf’s Problem? Sellout Culture.
  • Gloria Steinem on 50 Years of Ms. Magazine and the Feminist Movement
  • Crypto, Houses, Sneakers, Rolexes: How FOMO Drove the Economy
  • Democrats Need to Understand the Real Message San Francisco Sent
  • Your Thursday Evening Briefing
  • The Voice Notes We Shared
  • Thick-Cut Zucchini, Smashed Cucumbers and Grated Tofu
  • 如果你在唐山打人事件现场，会出手相助吗？
  • N.Y.C. Subway Rider Dies After Getting Stuck Between Platform and Train
  • Yellowstone Towns Had Big Summer Plans Until Floods Struck
  • Ukraine News: Zelensky Celebrates Support for Kyiv’s Path to E.U.
  • In Ukraine, Some Ethnic Hungarians Feel Ambivalence About the War
  • Man Confesses to Killing 2 Men Missing in the Amazon, Police Say
  • In New Textbooks, Hong Kong Was Never a British Colony
  • Union Strike in Tunisia Challenges President’s Rule
  • Federico Carboni Becomes Italy’s First Assisted Suicide
  • Kevin Spacey, Accused of Sexual Assault, Appears in British Court
  • The TikTok Star and Political Chameleon Vying for Colombia’s Presidency
  • A Chinese City May Have Used a Covid App to Block Protesters
  • BTS Ponders Its Future, and South Korea’s Economy Warily Takes Note
  • Your Friday Briefing
  • How a Globetrotting Family Learned to Love Soccer and the ‘Grey Wiggle’
  • Florida Didn’t Preorder Vaccine Doses for Youngest Children, State Says
  • Third American Fighter May Be Missing in Ukraine, State Department Says
  • Europe Offers Ukraine a Hope of Joining the E.U., but Not a Vast Arsenal
  • Your Friday Briefing: European Leaders Travel to Ukraine
  • These Polar Bears in Greenland Can Survive With Less Sea Ice
  • Little Amal, a Refugee Puppet Who Traveled Europe, Will Visit New York
  • Moscow says babies born in occupied Kherson will automatically get Russian citizenship.
  • Civilians Are Largely Stranded in Sievierodonetsk, in One of the War’s Deadliest Battles
  • Mass Shootings Are a Reality in Many Communities
  • A Businessman Is Acquitted in a Georgetown Admissions Trial
  • Synagogue Sues Florida, Saying Abortion Restrictions Violate Religious Freedoms
  • A Decade After DACA, the Rise of a New Generation of Undocumented Students
  • Yellowstone Flooding Is a Sign of What’s to Come for National Parks
  • Photos of Flooding Damage in Yellowstone
  • Bowling Green Fraternity Brothers Sentenced in Hazing Death of Student
  • Church Shooting in Alabama Leaves Two Dead and One Wounded
  • 4 Takeaways From Today’s Jan. 6 Hearing
  • YouTube Deletes Jan. 6 Video That Included Clip of Trump Sharing Election Lies
  • Homeland Security Department Will Make Changes to Its Disciplinary Process
  • Sotomayor Says Supreme Court Can ‘Regain the Public’s Confidence’
  • When Do Many At-Home Coronavirus Test Kits Expire?
  • Romney Accuses White House of Dissembling on Covid Aid
  • Who Is John Wood?
  • In a Split District in New Hampshire, Democrats Tune In and Republicans Tune Out
  • How a Religious Sect Landed Google in a Lawsuit
  • Bear Markets and Recessions Happen More Often Than You Think
  • Wall Street shudders as focus returns to recession risks.
  • Ukraine’s Highly Mobile Tech Work Force Hits the Road
  • Revlon Files for Bankruptcy
  • Trial of New Alzheimer’s Drug Reports Disappointing Results
  • BuzzFeed News Names Karolina Waclawiak as Top Editor
  • Sales at Art Basel Fair Don’t Show Signs of Downturn
  • In Twitter Meeting, Elon Musk Fields Questions From 8,000 Employees
  • USA Today to Remove 23 Articles After Investigation Into Fabricated Sources
  • Abbott Baby Formula Plant Again Stops Production, This Time Because of Flooding
  • Musk to Address Twitter All-Hands Meeting
  • Bank of England, fearing persistent inflation, raises rates yet again.
  • Germany Appeals to Save Gas After Russia Tightens Flows

  • Dog Owners Want More Space. Here’s Where They’re Finding It.
  • What the Fed’s Rate Hike Means for Credit Cards and Student Loans

News from the New York Times

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 17 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-17 04:06AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Australia news LIVE: Energy chief says crisis boosts case for renewables; Russia sanctions 121 Australians; National cabinet meets

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Financial Times: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Global stocks fall as central banks follow Fed decision to raise rates

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Coedo craft beer brewery donates percentage of sales to Ukraine
  • Tokyo set to issue same-sex couple certificates from Nov. 1

  • Asian shares gain after Fed assurance on rates lifts Wall Street
  • French, German, Italian leaders arrive in Kyiv
  • Australia commits to reducing greenhouse emissions by 43%
  • ‘Beautiful and airy,’ men’s lacy boxer briefs a surprise hit
  • TASTE OF LIFE/ Potage bonne femme: Post-2011 novel shows comfort food enriches our lives, like novels

  • VOX POPULI: Remembering noted Japanese literature scholar Donald Keene
  • VOX POPULI: Aid to Ukraine must not falter as Russian invasion drags on
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: Guardian: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia has strategically lost war, declares UK admiral, as Lavrov says no shame in war crimes
  • Golden State Warriors beat Celtics to win fourth NBA title in eight seasons
  • Stock markets plunge again as flurry of interest rate hikes fuels recession fears

  • It’s Groundhog Day … again! Why TV can’t get enough of time-loops

  • Dining across the divide: ‘Saying “I’m a socialist” doesn’t get you anywhere these days’
  • Glenda Jackson answers your questions: ‘I think that’s a gross insult about politicians and actors, frankly’
  • ‘Summer will be monstrous’: Barcelona wrestles with revival of mass tourism

  • Finally, I got Covid. Give me chicken soup, Marmite and drugs | Jay Rayner

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 17 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-17 03:22AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • How Hong Kong’s ‘Covid-19 heroes’ rose to the challenges they faced
  • Soaring interest rates: Hong Kong’s new mortgage borrowers and highly leveraged buyers are most at risk in the property market
  • Hong Kong’s biggest health crisis in 25 years: why did city stumble over Covid-19? Experts say it was too slow to change tactics, inflexible, put politics first
  • US pressures Iran by targeting Hong Kong and UAE firms
  • Incoming Hong Kong leader John Lee relies mostly on incumbent officials to fill ranks of his governing team
  • Hong Kong courts propose remote hearings for certain criminal trials, punishments for recording or broadcasting sessions
  • Veteran Hong Kong lawmaker Regina Ip set to become convenor of incoming leader John Lee’s de facto cabinet, sources say
  • Climate Change: Greater Bay Area holds the key to Hong Kong’s climate goals, delegates say at SCMP’s inaugural environmental summit
  • Suspects started clash between residents and protesters that led to elderly cleaner’s death, injured witness tells Hong Kong court
  • Company owned by Hong Kong justice chief Teresa Cheng’s husband sued for price fixing over air-conditioning works potentially worth HK$2 billion
  • Coronavirus: some patrons of Hong Kong bars adopt happy-go-lucky attitude in response to new rapid testing rule
  • Senior state officials laud ‘one country, two systems’ policy for Hong Kong, voice confidence in city’s prosperity and ‘quality democracy’
  • Going for a night out? What you need to know about Hong Kong’s new Covid rapid-test rule for bars and clubs
  • Dreaming big in a crisis: Hong Kong risk-takers didn’t let pandemic stop them starting, growing new enterprises
  • China appoints international affairs expert as new consul general in Brisbane
  • China’s beauty slimness obsession highlighted after woman’s weight drops to 25kg in a year with multiple organ failure and refuses treatment
  • China’s youth league to tackle graduate job pressure with advice, aid and a chance to ‘toughen’ up
  • White House adviser calls relationship with India ‘most important for the United States’
  • Why is China’s inflation rate low compared to the US, Europe and Britain?
  • Nicholas Burns, US ambassador to China, stresses need to find common ground amid tense relations
  • US security adviser says hard line on Russia is needed to dissuade China from similar moves
  • More than 90 per cent of oil slicks threatening global marine life caused by humans, US and Chinese scientists find
  • Who can fill the shoes of China’s Iron Lady Sun Chunlan on Politburo?
  • Western-style democracy behind wars, chaos and human misery, Chinese president tells top officials
  • Chinese scientists take ‘important step’ in stem cell therapy to treat Parkinson’s disease
  • US, China economic policies like night and day as Fed pushes interest rate skyward and PBOC eyes stabilisation
  • Taiwan admits paying American lobbyists to help establish closer US ties amid rising pressure from Beijing
  • Amazon, Cartier slap Chinese social media influencer, 8 others with lawsuits over trademark infringement, false advertising
  • China’s top Covid-19 fighter calls for all-in-one data portal like Europe’s EpiPulse
  • China vows private firms will receive ‘equal treatment’ in charm offensive to prop up struggling sector
  • Covid-19 in China: latest Beijing outbreak ‘easing’ after millions tested
  • Shanghai reopening: Disneyland ready for visitors once more as theme park awaits green light after Covid-19 lockdown
  • Chinese shipyard ready to launch Type 003 aircraft carrier, satellite images show
  • China debt holdings slashed by over 100 billion yuan in May, Beijing eager to ‘safeguard’ currency
  • China-India border: state media marks second anniversary of Galwan Valley clash in lead-up to new talks
  • Taiwan shows off its latest armoured vehicle, the CM-34 Clouded Leopard, as part of home-grown defence
  • WeChat suffers glitch that stopped users from paying for lunch deliveries before noon
  • Thai influencers charged with royal insult over Lazada adverts
  • How J.Y. Pillay helped Lee Kuan Yew turn Singapore Airlines into a global giant
  • South Korea and Japan look to move forward and ‘normalise’ information-sharing by keeping their tense history in the past
  • Protesters in India burn trains over military jobs plan
  • Why the US’ Indo-Pacific Economic Framework is really all about India
  • WTO reaches initial deal as India’s defiance tempered on vaccines, fishing, food security
  • Elimination of nuclear weapons an urgent need, UN chief warns
  • How would a US recession impact Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand amid surging inflation?
  • South Korea’s nuclear expansion plans in bid to meet climate targets
  • Migrants sent US$605 billion home in 2021, more by mobiles, UN finds
  • Thailand wants selfie-taking tourists to stop abusing famous tree on island
  • Coronavirus: Thailand mulls longer hours for pubs, bars as Covid cases drop
  • Sri Lanka’s economic crisis forces many to seek passport to a better life
  • Chinese destroyer on long-distance exercises in Sea of Japan, to deter ‘attack on Taiwan’
  • North Korea expands restoration at nuclear test site to second tunnel: report
  • Zelensky hologram references Star Wars: ‘We will defeat the empire too’
  • Omicron less likely to cause long Covid, UK study finds
  • Donald Trump unleashed mob after VP Mike Pence rejected election plot, Capitol riot panel hears
  • Amsterdam’s Schiphol Airport to cut flights over busy summer
  • Can polar bears survive climate change? Scientists see hopeful signs with discovery of new group
  • Kevin Spacey granted bail after ‘strenuously’ denying sex charges
  • World Cup 2022: tents in desert, no air cons, for fans as Fifa guests hog Qatar’s hotel rooms. An ‘opportunity’ to get an ‘authentic taste of Qatari camping’, official says
  • Russia blames Ukraine as Sievierodonetsk evacuation plan fails
  • Ukraine war: German, French, Italian leaders in Kyiv to show solidarity
  • UK signals it could exit Europe’s human rights convention after Rwanda deportation ruling
  • Vancouver’s housing crisis not caused by dirty money, inquiry finds
  • Tech stocks lift Hong Kong market while Asian equities languish as investors fret about Fed policy outlook
  • Chinese stocks emerge as safe haven from bear markets globally, as US rate rise eclipses Shanghai lockdown as bogeyman
  • Recession and inflation fears give way to a crisis of confidence in economic policymaking
  • HSBC teams up with Emma Raducanu to create new opportunities for young people around the world
  • HSBC leads clients to see beyond the norm with its art initiatives
  • Blockchain gaming unicorn Animoca Brands acquires educational start-up TinyTap to build tokenised learning platform
  • New home prices in China’s tier-1 cities rise at faster pace as relief policies, easing of pandemic controls boost market
  • How China’s new complaints procedures can prevent ‘green’ ESG investments from harming local communities
  • Yoga, meditation with Lululemon at Hong Kong’s M+ museum in summer programme focused on wellness, sustainability and healing
  • Netflix returns to Ghost in the Shell with new Japanese anime series
  • Chinese-American father-son cooks on YouTube channels Made with Lau and Hunger Pangs on how they’ve bonded over food
  • Budapest makeover: how modern architecture is transforming Hungary’s capital
  • A Murder Erased movie review: Hong Kong detective mystery so abysmally conceived it has to be seen to be believed
  • What’s really happening with BTS: a ‘temporary break’ to ‘explore solo projects’ but the group ‘are not on hiatus’ and ‘will remain active’, they and their label clarify
  • What to wear to lower the risk of having cataracts – sunglasses – and what to eat: goji berries for an ‘eye-brightening effect’, according to traditional Chinese medicine. Avoid smoking, too
  • POST EDIT: New Rolex Oyster Perpetual Lady-Datejust is perfect for the modern woman – and that little bit more luxurious
  • Video: Wushu world champion from Hong Kong explains how Chinese kung fu has broadened his horizons
  • Where is Hong Kong 80s beauty queen May Ng Yuen-fong today? The Miss Hong Kong runner-up married a wealthy business tycoon, lives in a mansion – and hasn’t seemed to age a day
  • Is the real British monarch in Australia? Here’s why some believe Edward IV’s reign was ‘illegitimate’, making farmer Michael Hastings and son Simon the ‘rightful heirs’ instead of Queen Elizabeth
  • Who is Elizabeth Hurley’s son and mirror image, Damian Hurley? The 20-year-old model’s godfathers include Hugh Grant and Elton John, and his dad is late millionaire and property heir, Steve Bing
  • 4 rare, unique whiskies with stand-out flavour and design, from the Macallan 72 Years Old in Lalique to decades-old bottles from Glenlivet and Yamasaki
  • How did Tony Blair’s son Euan become a billionaire? Once known as a ‘bad boy’ teen, his Multiverse edtech platform is now worth US$1.6 billion – and he’s even earned an MBE from Queen Elizabeth
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.
